feature: Proactive mode: keep the agent alive between user turns
what: With feature('PROACTIVE'||'KAIROS') the agent self-paces, takes initiative, and works autonomously between user inputs instead of idling after each turn. Activation is opt-in (--proactive / CLAUDE_CODE_PROACTIVE / set_proactive control RPC) on top of the build flag.
bridge: No proactive runtime/state-machine in the SDK (CC's own src/proactive/index.js is itself missing-source). Rebuild a harness loop on top of query() in streaming-input mode: drive the conversation with streamInput, inject synthetic prompts, and use interrupt/setPermissionMode for pause/resume. The whole subsystem is harness-level.

what: A setTimeout(0) scheduler enqueues a synthetic user message `<tick>HH:MM:SS</tick>` (mode:'prompt', priority:'later', isMeta:true) whenever the queue drains, so the model gets a recurring 'you're awake, what now?' nudge. No typed wakeup callback exists.
sdkSurface: Native ScheduleWakeup tool (delaySeconds, reason, /loop prompt + '<<autonomous-loop-dynamic>>' sentinel, sdk-tools.d.ts:2455) + SDKUserMessage priority/isSynthetic/shouldQuery via streamInput (sdk.d.ts:4184).
bridge: Self-wake / synthetic tick is the native ScheduleWakeup + synthetic-user-message surface.

what: A tool the model calls to wait between actions (minSleepDurationMs/maxSleepDurationMs settings; -1 = wait indefinitely for user input). Polls hasCommandsInQueue() and wakes within ~1s when a user/channel message arrives. SleepTool surface itself is spec 19.
sdkSurface: Native ScheduleWakeup (model-controlled pacing) + Monitor tool (wake-on-event, stop via TaskStop), both in the default query() tool list (runtime-verified 2026-06-16).
bridge: SleepTool pacing maps onto native ScheduleWakeup/Monitor.

what: A large verbatim system-prompt addendum teaching tick handling, pacing, first-wake greeting, bias-toward-action, terminalFocus calibration, and 'call Sleep if nothing to do'. Appended (not replacing) the default prompt when proactive is active.
sdkSurface: systemPrompt: { type:'preset', preset:'claude_code', append } or string[]
bridge: The SDK can carry the prompt text — append your reconstructed proactive section via systemPrompt append (or a custom string[] using SYSTEM_PROMPT_DYNAMIC_BOUNDARY). 'build' because the verbatim section is a CC asset you supply; the SDK provides the injection slot but not the content or the activation gating.

what: When the terminal is unfocused, a user-context line ('The terminal is unfocused — the user is not actively watching.') is injected so the model leans more autonomous; focused → more collaborative.
bridge: The SDK has no terminal-focus signal (it is a headless library with no TUI). A harness that owns a terminal can detect focus and prepend the context line to its injected SDKUserMessage content; pure-SDK consumers have no focus concept to wire.

what: Esc → pauseProactive() stops ticks; submitting input → resumeProactive() re-arms; context-blocked (set on API-error messages) suppresses ticks to avoid tick→error→tick runaway loops.
bridge: Pause/resume/error-backoff are properties of your tick loop, not the SDK. Use query.interrupt() to stop an in-flight turn and gate your own timer; watch SDKResultMessage error subtypes / SDKAPIRetryMessage to implement the context-blocked backoff.

feature: /goal — keep working until a condition is met (post-Feb)
what: Set a completion condition and let the agent keep working across turns until a fast-model evaluator confirms it. Post-February delta: Feb source has only autonomous-mode precursor comments, no /goal command or GoalTool. Bucketed by the surface doc as a thin Stop-hook + prompt wrapper.
bridge: Rebuild as a Stop-hook (HOOK_EVENTS 'Stop') that re-prompts unless a fast model judges the goal met, plus a maxTurns/maxBudgetUsd guard. SDK exposes the Stop hook and a model-swap path, but the goal-evaluator loop and the /goal command are yours to assemble.

what: A scheduled-trigger autonomous-assistant mode: `claude assistant [sessionId]` + `--assistant` daemon flag latch kairosActive=true, force --brief on, require trust-dialog acceptance, and init an assistant team. The assistant engine (src/assistant/index.js, gate.js, the verbatim system-prompt addendum) is missing-source.
bridge: Compose from SDK primitives: the assistant 'daemon' is a long-running query() driver (P2 runtime), brief-forcing and the system-prompt addendum go through systemPrompt, and team spawning maps to agents/Agent-tool. The engine itself is CC-internal and absent even from the leak, so it is reconstructed, not provided.

feature: Assistant session attach to a running bridge session (events API)
what: `claude assistant [sessionId]` re-attaches a local REPL as a viewer to a running remote/bridge session by paging GET ${BASE_API_URL}/v1/sessions/{sid}/events (OAuth + anthropic-beta: ccr-byoc-2025-07-29 + x-organization-uuid).
bridge: Depends on Anthropic's claude.ai session-events API (/v1/sessions/{id}/events) and CCR bridge infrastructure — a claude.ai-internal hosted service the SDK does not and cannot expose. Local-only session resume (resume/forkSession/sessionStore) is the SDK analogue but cannot attach to a cloud bridge session.

feature: Brief output channel (SendUserMessage tool) replaces plain text
what: When brief mode is on, the SendUserMessage (legacy Brief) tool becomes the user-visible output surface; plain text is hidden in a detail view. status:'normal'|'proactive' classifies messages and feeds downstream push routing.
bridge: Implement a SendUserMessage MCP tool via tool()/createSdkMcpServer plus the brief system-prompt section through systemPrompt append; your harness UI decides what is user-visible. The SDK has no built-in 'brief surface' concept — it always streams assistant text — so the visibility model is harness-level.

feature: Channels: bidirectional MCP-over-channel inbound + permission relay
what: Telegram/iMessage/Discord-style MCP servers push notifications/claude/channel events into a running session (wrapped as <channel source=…>…</channel> user messages) and relay tool-permission prompts (notifications/claude/channel/permission_request ↔ /permission), gated by tengu_harbor allowlist + --channels opt-in.
sdkSurface: Native channelsEnabled + allowedChannelPlugins settings + SDKControlChannelEnableRequest + SDKMessageOrigin kind:'channel' (sdk.d.ts:3700); permission relay via Elicitation hooks + canUseTool.
bridge: Inbound channel transport + permission relay are native (settings-gated); only the concrete adapter MCP servers are external.

what: Local cron tools persist 5-field cron tasks to .claude/scheduled_tasks.json; a scheduler tick fires prompts (one-shot fire-then-delete vs recurring recompute-and-stamp), with jitter, 7-day expiry, durable vs session-only, and missed-task surfacing.
sdkSurface: Native CronCreate/CronDelete/CronList tools (5-field cron, prompt, recurring, durable→.claude/scheduled_tasks.json, 7-day expiry; sdk-tools.d.ts:2430) + SessionCronSummary (sdk.d.ts).
bridge: Scheduler, cron store, and one-shot-vs-recurring logic are native.

what: RemoteTriggerTool creates/updates/runs/lists Routines on claude.ai (saved CC config run on Anthropic cloud via schedule / API /fire / GitHub triggers); backs the /schedule command.
bridge: Routines execute on Anthropic's cloud and are managed through claude.ai-internal endpoints; the SDK is a local headless library and exposes no Routines/RemoteTrigger API. Only local cron (32.5) is rebuildable. The cloud product is out of reach.

what: On KAIROS_GITHUB_WEBHOOKS, SubscribePRTool + /subscribe-pr subscribe to PR events delivered via a webhook-intake server. Tool, command, and the intake endpoint/auth are all missing-source.
bridge: The webhook-intake endpoint and PR-event delivery ride on the same Anthropic bridge/remote infrastructure as the events API (32.2) and are claude.ai-internal; even CC's own tool source is absent. A self-hosted GitHub-webhook receiver feeding query() is a different product, not parity with this surface.

what: On KAIROS_PUSH_NOTIFICATION, PushNotificationTool sends desktop notifications (+ phone push via Remote Control) for long-running / scheduled work; Brief status:'proactive' is the upstream eligibility signal. Tool source missing; routing claude.ai-side.
sdkSurface: Native PushNotification tool (status:'proactive'; output pushSent/localSent/disabledReason/hasFocus/idleSec, sdk-tools.d.ts:2519) + agentPushNotifEnabled/inputNeededNotifEnabled settings.
bridge: Both desktop and phone push legs are native (pushSent = mobile).

what: A bundled 'dream' skill (auto-enabled under bare KAIROS) consolidates memory in a forked agent; the disk-backed skill body (src/skills/bundled/dream.ts) is missing-source. Non-Kairos autoDream short-circuits when kairosActive.
bridge: Skills are first-class in the SDK (skills option, plugins, reloadSkills); ship your own dream skill via a plugin or skills directory and trigger it on a cron/idle tick. The consolidation logic and memory-write semantics are yours to author — the CC skill body isn't in the leak.

what: feature('DAEMON') compiles a `claude daemon [subcommand]` fast-path that boots a long-running supervisor process (daemonMain). Supervisor body, subcommands, daemonization, signal handling, idle timeout, and max-connection cap are all missing-source.
bridge: The SDK is a headless library with no daemon/server runtime. Build the supervisor yourself (a Node process managing query()/startup() sessions); the SDK supplies the per-session engine but not the long-running process, IPC, or lifecycle.

what: An internal lean fast-path (--daemon-worker) the supervisor uses to spawn child workers; deliberately skips enableConfigs()/initSinks() for perf. Worker registry, enumerated kinds, and restart policy are missing-source.
bridge: Worker process management is harness-level. Spawn workers however you like and have each run query()/startup(); spawnClaudeCodeProcess lets you customize how the underlying CC subprocess is launched (VM/container/remote), but the supervisor↔worker registry and IPC are yours to build.

what: SessionKind gains 'daemon'|'daemon-worker'; spawned children set CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_KIND so they register a JSON PID record under ~/.claude/sessions/<pid>.json (0o700), enabling `claude ps` listing and cleanup.
bridge: Process bookkeeping with no LLM involvement. If you want a `ps`-style view of SDK-driven sessions, write the PID/registry files yourself; the SDK offers session listing for its own transcript store (listSessions) but not OS-process registration.

feature: In-daemon hosted services (API / MCP / LSP multiplexing)
what: Whether the daemon hosts the Anthropic API client, MCP service host, or LSP service and multiplexes them across workers — all behind the absent daemonMain body.
bridge: If your daemon needs shared MCP/LSP/API services, wire them per-session through the SDK's mcpServers/query options (each query manages its own MCP connections). A shared multiplexed service tier is a custom architecture on top; the SDK has no service-host concept.

feature: Remote Control: drive a local REPL from claude.ai/code (web/mobile)
what: src/bridge/ is the CLI side of the bidirectional channel letting a local session be driven from claude.ai/code over HTTPS+WebSocket(v1) or HTTPS+SSE(v2). Product name 'Remote Control'; gate BRIDGE_MODE; GrowthBook tengu_ccr_bridge.
bridge: The entire bridge talks to Anthropic-hosted endpoints (/v1/environments/bridge, /v1/code/sessions, session_ingress WS/SSE) authenticated with claude.ai OAuth/JWT — a claude.ai-internal relay. The SDK exposes no Remote Control client/server; you cannot recreate the claude.ai/code control plane.

what: runBridgeLoop/bridgeMain: a standalone server that polls Anthropic for work and spawns child claude processes per remote session, with multi-session capacity, JWT session-ingress, and crash-recovery via bridge-pointer.json.
bridge: Its work-poll/spawn loop is bound to Anthropic's environments/code-sessions API and JWT ingress (claude.ai-internal). The generic idea of 'a server that spawns SDK sessions on demand' is buildable (see 33.1), but parity with the claude.ai-coupled Remote Control server is not.

what: Server-initiated control_request fan-out: initialize, set_model, set_max_thinking_tokens, set_permission_mode, interrupt — replied via control_response, with an outbound-only error path. Same SDKControlRequest/Response envelopes the SDK uses internally.
bridge: The control surface maps onto SDK Query methods (setModel, setPermissionMode, setMaxThinkingTokens, interrupt) in streaming mode. If you build your own transport you can translate inbound control frames to these calls — the methods are provided, but the bridge's claude.ai wire protocol/transport is not, so the glue is build.

what: X-Trusted-Device-Token keychain enrollment (POST /api/auth/trusted_devices, 10-min server gate) and per-session JWT bearer auth with refresh schedulers for the bridge channel.
bridge: Trusted-device enrollment and session-ingress JWTs are issued/validated by Anthropic's bridge backend (claude.ai-internal auth). The SDK has no hook into this auth flow; there is nothing to configure or rebuild client-side without the hosted endpoints.

what: A small set of slash commands (compact, clear, cost, summary, releaseNotes, files) allowed when driving a session from a phone via the bridge; local-jsx commands blocked.
bridge: Slash-command availability is a harness UI policy. With the SDK you enumerate commands via query.supportedCommands() and gate which ones your remote UI exposes; the specific safe-list is a convention you reimplement. Not meaningful without a remote UI (which itself is not-possible/34.1).

feature: CCR — agent runs in Anthropic-managed cloud container
what: Claude Code Remote: the agent executes in an Anthropic cloud container while the local CLI is a thin viewer/controller; setup via /web-setup, auto-attach via CCR_AUTO_CONNECT, outbound mirror via CCR_MIRROR. Server sets CLAUDE_CODE_REMOTE=true reshaping ~25 subsystems.
bridge: CCR containers, the /v1/sessions/ws subscribe channel, and the GitHub import-token / environment-provider endpoints are all Anthropic-hosted (claude.ai-internal). The SDK cannot provision or attach to a CCR container. Run your own cloud sandbox with the SDK if you need remote execution — that is a separate build, not CCR parity.

feature: Direct-connect server + cc:// client (claude server / open)
what: `claude server` exposes an HTTP+WebSocket session host (auto-generated sk-ant-cc-… token, idle/max-session limits, unix-socket option); `claude open cc://…` / `claude <cc-url>` connects. Self-hosted, not claude.ai-coupled.
bridge: The SDK ships the CLIENT side: DirectConnectTransport + parseDirectConnectUrl + DirectConnectError are exported, so connecting to a cc:// server is configurable. The SERVER (`claude server` host) is not an SDK API, so hosting the session is build (or use the CLI's own `claude server`). Client connect ≈ provided; the host runtime ≈ build.

what: `claude ssh <host>` deploys the binary to a Linux host, reverse-forwards local API auth over a unix socket, runs tools remotely while rendering UI locally; forwards --continue/--resume/--model to the remote spawn. Source (src/ssh/) not in leak.
bridge: No SSH-remote primitive in the SDK. spawnClaudeCodeProcess lets you launch the underlying CC process over a custom transport (e.g. an SSH-tunneled command), which is the closest hook; the deploy/reverse-auth-proxy plumbing is entirely yours to build.

what: A register-then-poll worker against Anthropic's SelfHostedRunnerWorkerService where the poll is the heartbeat. Fast-path present; runtime (src/self-hosted-runner/) absent.
bridge: The runner polls an Anthropic worker-service backend (claude.ai-internal); the SDK exposes no runner-registration/poll API. A bespoke job-runner that drives query() is buildable but is not parity with the hosted self-hosted-runner protocol.

feature: UDS inbox / peers (local Unix-domain-socket message bus)
what: feature('UDS_INBOX') starts a UDS messaging server (--messaging-socket-path or default tmp path), exporting $CLAUDE_CODE_MESSAGING_SOCKET so sibling tmux teammates exchange messages. Server source (udsMessaging.ts, commands/peers/) absent.
bridge: A local IPC bus between agent processes is harness-level. Build it with a UDS server of your own and feed inbound messages into each session via streamInput; the SDK provides no peer/messaging-socket transport (SendMessage agent-team messaging is a CC tool, not an SDK API).

what: `/ultrareview` (and reviewRemote.ts) creates a CCR session that runs the review prompt server-side — the primary CCR-launch user entrypoint.
bridge: It launches a CCR (cloud) session (35.1) and inherits the same claude.ai-internal dependency. A local equivalent (run a review prompt via query()) is trivially buildable, but the Teleported/remote-execution behavior is out of reach.

what: When set inside a CCR container, CLAUDE_CODE_REMOTE=true reshapes ~25 subsystems: heap bump, API timeout 300s→120s, hook events always included, voice disabled, plugin git-URL rewrite, memdir override, defaultMode constrained to acceptEdits|plan, keepalive suppression, etc.
bridge: These are internal behavior flips inside the CC binary when it runs as a CCR container guest; the variable is set by the CCR controller out-of-band and is meaningful only in that hosted context. The SDK does not run inside CCR and exposes none of these toggles individually.

what: feature('VOICE_MODE') hold-to-talk records mic audio, streams 16kHz/16-bit/mono PCM to Anthropic's voice_stream STT WebSocket, and splices interim+final transcripts into the prompt input. Anthropic-OAuth-only; gated by tengu_amber_quartz_disabled kill-switch + voiceEnabled setting.
bridge: Voice is a UI-input feature with no LLM-loop wiring (transcripts splice into the input buffer, then submit via the normal path). The SDK is headless and has zero audio/STT surface; the whole capture→STT→splice pipeline is a harness build on top of query()/streamInput.

feature: Microphone capture backends (cpal NAPI / arecord / SoX)
what: Audio capture via audio-capture-napi (cpal), Linux arecord, or SoX rec at 16kHz/mono/S16LE; macOS TCC permission probe; remote/homespace environments blocked.
bridge: Mic capture is pure OS/native integration the SDK does not touch. Reuse a native audio module yourself; nothing in the SDK helps or hinders. Note CC blocks voice under CLAUDE_CODE_REMOTE, reinforcing that this is local-only client tooling.

what: WebSocket to wss://{api}/api/ws/speech_to_text/voice_stream (encoding=linear16, sample_rate=16000, endpointing/utterance params, optional Deepgram Nova3), OAuth bearer, keyterms, KeepAlive/CloseStream control frames, TranscriptText/Endpoint/Error wire messages.
bridge: The voice_stream STT endpoint is an Anthropic-hosted service (claude.ai-internal, OAuth-only) the SDK does not surface — there is no SDK STT API or endpoint config. You could point your own STT pipeline at a third-party provider, but that is not parity with the CC voice_stream backend.

feature: Voice UI: push-to-talk key handling, indicator, splicing, keybinding
what: voice:pushToTalk (default space), HOLD_THRESHOLD/release-timeout FSM, VoiceIndicator 'listening…'/shimmer, live interim-transcript splicing into the prompt buffer, /voice toggle command, VoiceModeNotice.
bridge: All TUI/keybinding/indicator/splicing concerns belong to the terminal UI, which the headless SDK does not provide. Deferred to the UI cluster (C11) as a P3 client-UI feature; the SDK contributes nothing to key handling or input-buffer rendering.
